title: Scroll Triggers, Grid Lanes, Turbulence, Fluid Cursor & Anchor Menus
description: Five fresh UI drops: native scroll-triggered CSS animation, Grid Lanes masonry, SVG turbulence text, a WebGL fluid cursor, and zero-JS anchor-positioned menus.

drops.title: Scroll-Triggered Reveals
drops.description: Cards fire a keyframe animation once when crossing a scroll boundary, then reverse it on the way back up.
drops.concept: Chrome 145 ships a new CSS primitive, animation-trigger + timeline-trigger, that fires a keyframe animation forward or backward exactly at a scroll boundary crossing — distinct from continuous scroll-linked (animation-timeline: view()) animation. This drop reimplements the same forward/backward-at-a-boundary behavior with IntersectionObserver + CSS transitions (guaranteed to run everywhere today) while layering the real native syntax behind an @supports (animation-trigger: --t) block for browsers that already ship it.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers Blog — "CSS scroll-triggered animations are coming!"

interaction.mouse: Scroll the page to trigger each card
interaction.touch: Scroll to trigger; use the tuning panel on wider screens

drops.title: Grid Lanes Masonry
drops.description: Tiles drop bumper-to-bumper into whichever lane is currently shortest, DOM order preserved for accessibility.
drops.concept: WebKit's new display: grid-lanes lays out masonry-style grids natively, placing each item into the shortest lane so far (a 'flow-tolerance' threshold prevents items jumping lanes for tiny height differences) while keeping DOM order intact for tab/reader order — unlike JS masonry libraries that reorder the DOM. This drop reimplements the exact shortest-lane-first algorithm in vanilla JS as the default renderer, with a progressive-enhancement CSS path that switches to the real grid-lanes/masonry engine when a browser supports it.

interaction.mouse: Drag the lane/gap sliders and click Regenerate

drops.title: Organic Text Distortion
drops.description: SVG feTurbulence-generated Perlin noise warps glyphs like wet paper, glass, or melting wax.
drops.concept: An off-screen SVG <filter> chains feTurbulence (Perlin/fractal noise generator) into feDisplacementMap, which reads the noise's red/green channels as per-pixel x/y displacement for the text underneath. Animating baseFrequency each frame gives the glyphs a continuous organic wobble; hovering a word temporarily boosts the displacement scale for an interactive 'melt' effect.
drops.source: DEV Community (hexshift) — "Creating Organic Textures with SVG Filter Distortions"

interaction.mouse: Hover a word to intensify its distortion
interaction.touch: Tap a word; use the tuning panel on wider screens

drops.title: Fluid Cursor Trail
drops.description: A GPU Navier-Stokes solver reacts to the pointer in real time — advection, vorticity, pressure projection.
drops.concept: A chain of WebGL fragment shaders running on ping-pong framebuffers: semi-Lagrangian advection samples velocity/dye backward along the flow field, a curl pass plus vorticity-confinement force keeps the fluid swirling instead of just diffusing, and a Jacobi-iteration pressure-projection pass enforces incompressibility before subtracting the pressure gradient back into velocity. Pointer movement injects a Gaussian 'splat' of color and velocity into both textures every frame.

interaction.mouse: Move or drag to stir the fluid
interaction.touch: Drag with one or more fingers

drops.title: Zero-JS Anchor Menus
drops.description: A File/Edit/View menu bar with cascading submenus built entirely on native Popover + Anchor Positioning.
drops.concept: popovertarget wires a button to a <menu popover> with zero JS for open/close, light-dismiss, and Escape handling. position-anchor + position-area tie each popover to its trigger button's anchor-name, cascading nested submenus sideways off their own trigger item. transition-behavior: allow-discrete plus @starting-style gives the popover a real enter/exit CSS transition instead of an instant display swap — the pattern being pitched as a full replacement for Floating UI/Popper/Tippy.
drops.source: coryrylan.com — "Dropdown Menus with HTML Popovers and CSS Anchor Positioning"

interaction.mouse: Click File/Edit/View, then click items with an arrow to open their submenu
interaction.touch: Tap to open; tap outside to dismiss
